steps.title: Extract circle F's radius from its equation
steps.content: The equation $(x + 7)^2 + (y + 7)^2 = 36$ is in standard form $(x - h)^2 + (y - k)^2 = r^2$. The right side equals $r_F^2 = 36$, so the radius of circle F is $r_F = \sqrt{36} = 6$.

steps.title: Calculate circle G's radius from the given relationship
steps.content: Circle G has the same center as circle F. Since its radius is 2 units greater than circle F's radius, we have $r_G = 6 + 2 = 8$.

steps.title: Find the constant p in circle G's equation
steps.content: Circle G's equation is $(x + 7)^2 + (y + 7)^2 = p$, where the right side equals $r_G^2$. Therefore, $p = 8^2 = 64$.
